title: [Flatten Memref] The pass `-flatten-memref` fails for global memrefs intialized with DenseElementsAttr (hex encoded)
body:
The `-flatten-memref` pass fails (seg fault) when a global value is initialized with a DenseElementsAttr (hex encoded).

Here is the (not so exciting) program :

```
module {
  memref.global "private" @conv_weight : memref<8x3x5x5xf32> = dense<"">
  memref.global "private" @conv_bias : memref<8xf32> = dense<[0.014328233, 0.0658490136, 0.0758575574, 0.0186426975, -0.00416149339, 0.0422742292, -0.0559486598, 0.0224983171]>
  
  func.func @relu4d_0(%arg0: memref<1x8x64x64xf32>) -> memref<1x8x64x64xf32> attributes {itypes = "_", otypes = "_"} {
    %wow = memref.get_global @conv_weight : memref<8x3x5x5xf32>
    %alloc = memref.alloc() {name = "Z"} : memref<1x8x64x64xf32>
    %cst = arith.constant 0.000000e+00 : f32
    %0 = arith.cmpf uno, %cst, %cst : f32
    %c0 = arith.constant 0 : index
    %c1 = arith.constant 1 : index
    %c1_0 = arith.constant 1 : index
    scf.for %arg1 = %c0 to %c1 step %c1_0 {
      %c0_1 = arith.constant 0 : index
      %c8 = arith.constant 8 : index
      %c1_2 = arith.constant 1 : index
      scf.for %arg2 = %c0_1 to %c8 step %c1_2 {
        %c0_3 = arith.constant 0 : index
        %c64 = arith.constant 64 : index
        %c1_4 = arith.constant 1 : index
        scf.for %arg3 = %c0_3 to %c64 step %c1_4 {
          %c0_5 = arith.constant 0 : index
          %c64_6 = arith.constant 64 : index
          %c1_7 = arith.constant 1 : index
          scf.for %arg4 = %c0_5 to %c64_6 step %c1_7 {
            %1 = memref.load %arg0[%arg1, %arg2, %arg3, %arg4] : memref<1x8x64x64xf32>
            %2 = arith.cmpf ugt, %1, %cst : f32
            %3 = arith.select %2, %1, %cst : f32
            %4 = arith.select %0, %cst, %3 : f32
            memref.store %4, %alloc[%arg1, %arg2, %arg3, %arg4] : memref<1x8x64x64xf32>
          }
        }
      }
    }
    return %alloc : memref<1x8x64x64xf32>
  }
  
  
}
```

and here is the stack dump:

```

#24 0x000055b59368950c mlir::MlirOptMain(int, char**, llvm::StringRef, llvm::StringRef, mlir::DialectRegistry&) (./circt-opt+0x470f50c)

#26 0x000055b591393817 main (./circt-opt+0x2419817)
#27 0x00007fc16ec40bfc __libc_start_call_main ./csu/../sysdeps/nptl/libc_start_call_main.h:74:3
#28 0x00007fc16ec40cb5 call_init ./csu/../csu/libc-start.c:128:20
#29 0x00007fc16ec40cb5 __libc_start_main@GLIBC_2.2.5 ./csu/../csu/libc-start.c:347:5

Segmentation fault         ./circt-opt -flatten-memref ~/allo-test/small-test.mlir
```

As i tried digging into this, it seems like this issue is from the snippet below from [this](https://github.com/llvm/circt/blob/main/lib/Transforms/FlattenMemRefs.cpp) :
```cpp
   auto cstAttr =
        llvm::dyn_cast_or_null<DenseElementsAttr>(op.getConstantInitValue());

    SmallVector<Attribute> flattenedVals;
    for (auto attr : cstAttr.getValues<Attribute>())
      flattenedVals.push_back(attr);
  
```
We didn't check if `cstAttr` is `null`, instead called `getValues` which looks like a `seg fault` to me.

If this is something that hasn't been looked at already, I would like work on this.
